purpose: Section-specific revise prompt for revising a draft solution field that failed its rubric. Composed at runtime with system_prompts/revise.json.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE REVISING: solution

The solution field is { approach: string, key_benefits: string[] }. The previous draft failed at least one dimension of the V1 solution rubric. Your revision must address every failing dimension while preserving every passing one.

DIMENSION-BY-DIMENSION REVISION GUIDANCE

If names_concrete_artefact failed (≤ 1):
  - Approach is too abstract. Pick a specific path (e.g. `eval/refresh/config.yaml`), a specific file shape (named fields, expected types), and what goes in the artefact. Use the catalogue's existing conventions (eval/, docs/adrs/) as the precedent — do not invent novel paths if a sibling already established a convention.

If names_enforcement_mechanism failed (≤ 1):
  - Approach gestures at enforcement but doesn't name the gate. Add: WHICH gate (pre_merge CI, ARB quarterly review, pre_deploy check), WHAT it triggers on, WHAT it blocks. The reader should be able to point at the gate.

If derives_from_statement failed (≤ 1):
  - Approach introduces concepts not implied by the statement. Re-read the statement carefully. Strip anything that's an invention of the approach (e.g. a 'verification chain', a novel governance mechanism) and reconfine the approach to implementing what the statement mandates.

If benefits_map_to_problem failed (≤ 1):
  - Benefits are generic. For each benefit, ask: 'which problem.example does this address?' If no answer, rewrite the benefit to name the specific failure mode it prevents. Use the sibling solution sections as the calibration — they typically have benefits that read like 'catches the X failure that simpler controls miss' or 'closes the loop with sibling principle Y'.

If vendor_neutral_implementation failed (≤ 1):
  - Strip vendor-specific tooling from approach. If a vendor tool is genuinely important to mention, move it to a framework_mappings note (not in this draft). The approach should be implementable on any stack.

If parallel_form_with_siblings failed (≤ 1):
  - Compare against sibling solution sections directly. Match approach length (4-8 sentences), benefits count (3-5), benefit length, vocabulary, tense. Do not invent new framing if a sibling has established the convention.

WHAT TO PRESERVE

The rubric scores tell you which dimensions passed. Preserve those. If only benefits_map_to_problem failed, you may only need to rewrite the benefits — don't touch the approach.
user_template:
The previous solution draft failed its rubric. Revise it.

<principle_id>{{ principle_id }}</principle_id>

<aws_anchor>
  <best_practice>{{ aws_bp }}</best_practice>
  <implementation_step>
    <number>{{ aws_step_number }}</number>
    <title>{{ aws_step_title }}</title>
    <verbatim_text>{{ aws_step_verbatim }}</verbatim_text>
  </implementation_step>
</aws_anchor>

<statement>
  <title>{{ statement_title }}</title>
  <description>{{ statement_description }}</description>
</statement>

<problem>
  <description>{{ problem_description }}</description>
  <examples>{{ problem_examples }}</examples>
</problem>

<siblings_under_same_bp>
{{ sibling_solutions }}
</siblings_under_same_bp>

<failing_draft>
  <approach>{{ candidate_approach }}</approach>
  <key_benefits>{{ candidate_key_benefits }}</key_benefits>
</failing_draft>

<rubric_scores>
  <names_concrete_artefact score="{{ score_names_concrete_artefact }}">{{ justification_names_concrete_artefact }}</names_concrete_artefact>
  <names_enforcement_mechanism score="{{ score_names_enforcement_mechanism }}">{{ justification_names_enforcement_mechanism }}</names_enforcement_mechanism>
  <derives_from_statement score="{{ score_derives_from_statement }}">{{ justification_derives_from_statement }}</derives_from_statement>
  <benefits_map_to_problem score="{{ score_benefits_map_to_problem }}">{{ justification_benefits_map_to_problem }}</benefits_map_to_problem>
  <vendor_neutral_implementation score="{{ score_vendor_neutral_implementation }}">{{ justification_vendor_neutral_implementation }}</vendor_neutral_implementation>
  <parallel_form_with_siblings score="{{ score_parallel_form_with_siblings }}">{{ justification_parallel_form_with_siblings }}</parallel_form_with_siblings>
</rubric_scores>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract — same shape as the generate output, drop-in replacement.
